title: Vines Of Fate
subtitle: Climb the vine of destiny. Try not to look down. Definitely don't look down.
team: Global Game Jam 2023 · 48 hours · team of 3
specs: Developer, sound effects, and — yes — the voice

gallery.caption: Two faces in the dark. Friendly? Unclear. Keep climbing.
The vines glow so you can see where fate wants you to go.
Every run grows a different vine — like a life, procedurally.
Red means obstacle. Red means think fast.

body: Vines Of Fate was our 48-hour sprint at Global Game Jam 2023, and the pitch is dangerously poetic: everybody gets one vine in this life — now go grow yours. You climb a first-person tangle of glowing vines that represent your destiny, dodging the obstacles trying to knock you off it. Every run generates a brand-new vine, because (deep breath) no two lives are the same. We were very tired and very sincere.

Three of us, 48 hours. I was on development, built all the sound effects, and — because someone had to and nobody stopped me — I did the voiceover too. So if you hear a mysterious narrator gently guiding you toward your fate: hi. That's me. Doing my Serious Voice.

The look came together almost by accident. Pitch-dark scene, a few emissive materials on the vines, and suddenly the whole thing felt like climbing through a neon dream. It's the kind of thing you only find when you're too sleep-deprived to overthink it. Is it a complete game? No. Is it a vibe? Absolutely. Some of my favourite work happens in the 48-hour pressure cooker, and this one still makes me smile.
